# Tab 1

**Ara X DayDreamers College Activation Proposal**

**DayDreamers** will organize a coordinated campus tour across leading universities in the United States to drive awareness and adoption of Ara among student builders and technical communities.

---

**Offering** 

**Quote: 100,000 USD**   
*(Inclusive of all costs except the prize pool)*

**Part A. Deliverables**

**1\. Campus tour across 6 universities** 

We partner with local student communities and technical clubs at each university to **organize hackathons and builder events.**

**Operational Support**

* On-site operators from the DayDreamers team to ensure consistency across events  
*  **1 to 3 staff members** to support each campus event activation.

**Target Universities**

* Stanford University  
* Johns Hopkins University  
* Harvard University  
* Massachusetts Institute of Technology  
* University of Pennsylvania  
* New York University  
* Columbia University  
* University of California, Berkeley


---

**2\. Ara Builder Community with Top Universities** 

Alongside the campus tour, DayDreamers will help build the **Ara builder community on Discord or Slack** for the hackathon. 

This online community will serve as a **central hub connecting students, builders, and organizers** across all participating universities.

**Key objectives include:**

* Onboarding participants from each campus activation into a shared global community  
* Enabling collaboration between students from different universities  
* Sharing tutorials, build resources, and project support for Ara  
* Highlighting projects and demos created during the hackathons  
* Maintaining engagement after each campus event

The goal is to transform the campus tour from a series of isolated events into a **builder network around Ara.**

This community layer helps sustain engagement, support new builders, and extend the impact of the campus tour beyond the physical events.  
---

**3\. Event format**

**We recommend running** each stop as a repeatable **6-hour Ara builder sprint.**

Suggested format:

| Activity | Duration |
| :---- | :---: |
| Kickoff & Live Ara Demo | 30 minutes |
| Idea Formation & Team Matching | 45 minutes |
| Guided Build Session | 3 hours |
| Mentor Feedback | 45 minutes |
| Demos & Judging | 60 minutes |
| Closing & Community Onboarding | 30 minutes |

**Why this format works:**

* Short enough for strong student turnout  
* Long enough for real product usage and demos  
* Easy to repeat across campuses  
* Lighter operationally than a 24 to 48 hour hackathon  
* Well suited to a multi-campus roadshow

---

**4\. End to end event execution** 

**We manage the entire process:**

* University outreach  
* Student organizer coordination  
* Event planning  
* Marketing and promotion  
* Onsite operations  
* Post event reporting

The Ara team simply has to review the materials.

**Media Outreach**  
DayDreamers will conduct media outreach to relevant publications. Any resulting media coverage is dependent on editorial decisions made by third party media organizations and cannot be guaranteed.  
---

**Part B Timeline** 

The tour will run **from April to June**.

**Proposed Dates**

* 11 April/ 12 April   
* 18 April/ 19 April   
* 23 May/ 24 May   
* 30 May/ 31 May  
* 6 June/ 7 June   
* 13 June/ 14 June

Because **May is typically exam season in US universities**, the events will be distributed across the timeline to maximize attendance.

Expected cadence: **1–3 campus activations per week**.  
---

**Part C Community Contacts** 

We have direct connections with organizers across major university hacker communities.

These organizers run hackathons, coding clubs, and student tech communities.

| Full Name | Organization / School / Company |
| :---- | :---- |
| Aidan Gu | UC Santa Cruz |
| Jung Hyun Andrew Kim | SFU Surge |
| Ethan Ng | Deltahacks, McMaster University |
| James Cao | Hackathons CanAra |
| Nitish Chaudhary | Codeforge |
| Harshit Malviya | Shaheed Sukhdev College of Business Studies |
| Gregory Ramirez | University of South Florida |
| Leon Xu | Johns Hopkins University |
| Elizabeth Wong | Concordia University / HackConcordia |
| Roland Yang | University of California, Los Angeles |
| Anna Belenko | Computer Science Club / Brooklyn College |
| Matthew Shi | TidalTAMU, Texas A\&M University |
| Heli KArakia | University of California, Santa Cruz |

---

## **Part D. Previous Event Experience** 

DayDreamers has organized **450+ global events, including hackathons, demo days, and community gatherings** focused on technology adoption.

**Relevant experience:**

 **Stanford Founder Programs**       
**1,000+ founders,** including a demo day with **90 startups, 1,500+** attendees**, and 300** investors**.**

 **Stanford AI \+ Learning Differences hackathon**   
**200 participants.**

 **Johns Hopkins ITGL hackathon**  
**40+ participants, 8 teams, 75%** intending to **continue their projects.**

 **Goodnotes hackathon**  
**73 participants** from **15+ countries,** producing **21 projects.**

 **PolyU AI hackathon**  
**50+ students** and **20+ participants** mentored**.**

 **Magic Loops, represented** the **product** at **hackathons** and **workshops,** driving **adoption** among **100+** early **users.**

 **Stanford Research,** drafted **white paper** and **research proposals** on **AI, entrepreneurship, and learning.**

**50+ Hackathons/ Demo Day**  
**400+ Curated Dinners in 17+ Cities**
